mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that reduces discount rates for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in nonfarm payrolls below 100k signals weak labor market conditions that depress corporate earnings expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of instances where PAYEMS declines exceed 100k, with t-test p < 0.05 for mean return difference.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in real consumer spending (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) indicates strong demand momentum, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC for the combined RSAFS-HOUST signal drops below 0.05 over any 3-year window, or 60d returns show negative correlation with the composite index.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly contraction in industrial production (INDPRO) below its 12-month moving average signals weakening supply chain capacity, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns exceed 1.5% in 40%+ of instances where INDPRO declines below its 12mo MA, with t-test p > 0.05 for mean return difference.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI inflation reading above 2.5% triggers central bank policy tightening expectations, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are positive in > 50% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 2.5, with t-test p > 0.05 for mean return difference.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate (UNRATE) above 8.5% combined with declining payrolls (PAYEMS) signals deteriorating labor market conditions, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC for the UNRATE-PAYEMS interaction drops below 0.03 over any 2-year window, or 5d returns show positive correlation with the composite index.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly GDP growth rate (GDPC1) above 3% indicates strong economic expansion, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are negative in 50%+ of instances where GDPC1 exceeds 3%, with t-test p > 0.05 for mean return difference.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in industrial production (INDPRO) combined with rising oil prices (CPIAUCSL) signals supply-side constraints, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC for the INDPRO-CPIAUCSL interaction drops below 0.02 over any 3-year window, or 20d returns show positive correlation with the composite index.
